edition.name: Mongoose Traveller 2e
edition.description: Mongoose Traveller 2nd Edition (2022 Core Rulebook): six characteristics run through a Characteristic Modifier table and a unified 2D + DM vs 8+ task system; twelve careers with three assignments each, per-term Qualification / Survival / Events / Advancement / Skills, and mustering-out benefits.
extractionNotes: Extracted from Traveller 2022 Core Rulebook 20-02-2026.pdf (text-layer). Printed page = PDF page (offset 0) except the careers chapter where PDF Image pN markers run +1. TRAVELLER CREATION printed pp.8-57; SKILLS AND TASKS pp.58-72.

rules.$attributeCaps: Core p.9: characteristics roll 2D (2-12), assigned 0-15; human maximum 15. Floor 0 (a characteristic reduced to 0 triggers an ageing crisis / death, Core p.49).

mongoose.$startAge: Core p.8: creation begins at age 18.
mongoose.$termLengthYears: Core p.11-12: each career term lasts four years.
mongoose.$defaultTaskTarget: Core p.61: if no difficulty is listed, assume Average (8+).
mongoose.$characteristicDmBands: Core p.9 Characteristic Modifiers table: 0 -> -3, 1-2 -> -2, 3-5 -> -1, 6-8 -> +0, 9-11 -> +1, 12-14 -> +2, 15+ -> +3. Top band open-ended (encoded max 99).
mongoose.$backgroundSkillBase: Core p.10: background skills = EDU DM + 3 (0-6). Base 3; engine adds the EDU characteristic DM.
mongoose.$backgroundSkills: Core p.10 background skill list (17 skills), all gained at level 0.
mongoose.$preCareer: Core pp.16-17 University / Military Academy. Populated with entry checks and graduation benefits in the pre-career task.
mongoose.$draft: Core p.20 Draft table (1D): 1 Navy (any), 2 Army (any), 3 Marine (any), 4 Merchant (merchant marine), 5 Scout (any), 6 Agent (law enforcement).

qualification.$qualification: Core p.22: INT 6+. DM-1 for every previous career (standard rule; no career-specific extra).

agent.$assignments: Core p.22 Survival & Advancement (by assignment) + assignment specialist 1D skill columns.

skillTables.$rule: Core p.22 Skills and Training tables

ranks.$rule: Core p.22 Ranks and Bonuses. Agent has two ladders: 'Law Enforcement' and shared 'Intelligence/Corporate'. Civilian career - no officer ladder.

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

An investigation takes on a dangerous turn. Roll Investigate 8+ or Streetwise 8+. If you fail, roll on the Mishap table. If you succeed, increase one of these skills by one level: Deception, Jack-of-all-Trades, Persuade or Tactics.
You complete a mission for your superiors and are suitably rewarded. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll from this career.
You establish a network of contacts. Gain D3 Contacts.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to increase any one skill you already have by one level.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

You go undercover to investigate an enemy. Roll Deception 8+. If you succeed, roll immediately on the Rogue or Citizen Events table and make one roll on any Specialist skill table for that career. If you fail, roll immediately on the Rogue or Citizen Mishap table.
You go above and beyond the call of duty. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
You are given specialist training in vehicles. Gain one of Drive 1, Flyer 1, Pilot 1 or Gunner 1.
You are befriended by a senior agent. Either increase Investigate by one level or DM+4 to an advancement roll thanks to their aid.
Your efforts uncover a major conspiracy against your employers. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.

A criminal or other figure under investigation offers you a deal. Accept and you leave this career without further penalty (although you lose the Benefit roll as normal). Refuse and you must roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result. You gain an Enemy and one level in any skill you choose.

An investigation goes critically wrong or leads to the top, ruining your career. Roll Advocate 8+. If you succeed, you may keep the Benefit roll from this term. If you roll 2, you must take the Prisoner career in your next term.
You learn something you should not know and people want to kill you for it. Gain an Enemy and Deception 1.
Your work ends up coming home with you and someone gets hurt. Choose one of your Contacts, Allies or family members and roll twice on the Injury table for them, taking the lower result.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

qualification.$qualification: END 5+; DM-1 for every previous career; DM-2 if you are aged 30 or more (Core p.24)

army.$assignments: Core p.24 Assignments; Survival & Advancement by assignment

skillTables.$rule: Core p.24 Skills and Training tables

officer.title: General
officer.benefit: SOC 10 or SOC +1, whichever is higher

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
You are assigned to a planet with a hostile or wild environment. Gain one of Vacc Suit 1, Engineer 1, Animals (riding or training) 1 or Recon 1.
You are assigned to an urbanised planet torn by war. Gain one of Stealth 1, Streetwise 1, Persuade 1 or Recon 1.
You are given a special assignment or duty in your unit. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You are thrown into a brutal ground war. Roll EDU 8+ to avoid injury; if you succeed, you gain one level in Gun Combat or Leadership.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to increase any one skill you already have by one level.
Surrounded and outnumbered by the enemy, you hold out until relief arrives. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
You are assigned to a peacekeeping role. Gain one of Admin 1, Investigate 1, Deception 1 or Recon 1.
Your commanding officer takes an interest in your career. Either gain Tactics (military) 1 or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
You display heroism in battle. You may gain a promotion or a commission automatically.

mishaps.text: Severely injured in action (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
Your unit is slaughtered in a disastrous battle, for which you blame your commander. Gain them as an Enemy as they have you removed from the service.

You are sent to a very unpleasant region (jungle, swamp, desert, icecap, urban) to battle against guerrilla fighters and rebels. You are discharged because of stress, injury or because the government wishes to bury the whole incident. Increase Recon or Survival by one level but also gain the rebels as an Enemy.

You discover that your commanding officer is engaged in some illegal activity, such as weapon smuggling. You can join their ring and gain them as an Ally before the inevitable investigation gets you discharged or you can co-operate with the military police - the official whitewash gets you discharged anyway but you may keep your Benefit roll from this term of service.
You are tormented by or quarrel with an officer or fellow soldier. Gain that officer as a Rival as they drive you out of the service.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

qualification.$qualification: Core p.26 Qualification: EDU 5+; DM-1 for every previous career (standard, applied globally). No career-specific extra DM. Cross-checked vs Career Summary Table Core p.21.

citizen.$basicTrainingFromAssignment: Core p.18: Citizens and Drifters use their Assignment Skills for basic training.
citizen.$assignments: Core p.26 Assignments (Corporate/Worker/Colonist); Survival & Advancement cross-checked vs Career Summary Table (Core pp.20-21).

skillTables.$rule: Core p.26 Skills and Training tables
skillTables.$basicTraining: Core p.26 (recap p.19): Citizen basic-training exception -- gain skills from the chosen Assignment table at level 0, not Service Skills.

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

Political upheaval strikes your homeworld and you are caught up in the revolution. Gain either Advocate 1, Persuade 1, Explosives 1 or Streetwise 1. Roll whichever skill you chose 8+. If you succeed you come out on the winning side and gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll. Fail and you suffer DM-2 to your next Survival roll.
You spend time maintaining and using heavy vehicles, either as part of your job or as a hobby. Increase Mechanic, Drive, Electronics, Flyer or Engineer by one level.
Your business expands, your corporation grows or the colony thrives. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 10+ to gain any one skill of your choice at level 1.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

You learn something you should not have - a corporate secret, a political scandal - which you can profit from illegally. If you choose to do so, then you gain DM+1 to a Benefit roll from this career and gain Streetwise 1, Deception 1 or a criminal Contact. If you refuse, you gain nothing.
You are rewarded for your diligence or cunning. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
You gain experience in a technical field as a computer operator or surveyor. Increase Electronics or Engineer by one level.
You befriend a superior in the corporation or the colony. Gain an Ally. Either gain Diplomat 1 or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
You rise to a position of power in your colony or corporation. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
You are harassed and your life ruined by a criminal gang. Gain the gang as an Enemy.
Hard times caused by a lack of interstellar trade costs you your job. Lose one SOC.

Your business is investigated by the planetary authorities (or your colony suffers interference from interests offworld). Co-operate and the business or colony is shut down but you gain DM+2 to the qualification roll for your next career as a reward for your aid. Refuse and gain an Ally.
A revolution, attack or other unusual event throws your life into chaos, forcing you to leave the planet. Roll Streetwise 8+. If you succeed, increase any skill you have by one level.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

qualification.$qualification: Automatic (no roll; always open). Drifter is always available regardless of number of previous careers; the standard DM-1-per-career qualification penalty does not gate entry (Core p.28; Career Summary Table Core p.21).

drifter.$basicTrainingFromAssignment: Core p.18: Citizens and Drifters use their Assignment Skills for basic training.

skillTables.$rule: Core p.28 Skills and Training tables
skillTables.$note: Drifter has NO Advanced Education table (advancedEducation is null; advancedEducationEduMin is null). Basic-training exception: a Drifter's first-career basic training grants the chosen Assignment skill table's skills at level 0, NOT the Service Skills (Core p.28; recap Core p.20).

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
A patron offers you a chance at a job. If you accept, you gain DM+4 to your next qualification roll but you owe that patron a favour.
You pick up a few useful skills here and there. Gain one level of Jack-of-all-Trades, Survival, Streetwise or Melee (any).
You manage to scavenge something of use. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You encounter something unusual. Go to the Life Events table and have an Unusual Event.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
You are attacked by enemies. Gain an Enemy if you do not have one already and roll either Melee 8+, Gun Combat 8+ or Stealth 8+ to avoid a roll on the Injury table.

You are offered a chance to take part in a risky but rewarding adventure. If you accept, roll 1D: On a 1-2, you are injured or arrested; either roll on the Injury table or take the Prisoner career in your next term. On 3-4, you survive, but gain nothing. On a 5-6, you succeed. Gain DM+4 to one Benefit roll.
Life on the edge hones your abilities. Increase any skill you already have by one level.
You are forcibly drafted. Roll for the Draft next term.
You thrive on adversity. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).
You run afoul of a criminal gang, corrupt bureaucrat or other foe. Gain an Enemy.
You suffer from a life-threatening illness. Reduce your END by 1.

Betrayed by a friend. One of your Contacts or Allies betrays you, ending your career. That Contact or Ally becomes a Rival or Enemy. If you have no Contacts or Allies, then you are betrayed by someone you never saw coming and still gain a Rival or Enemy. In addition, roll 2D. If you roll 2, you must take the Prisoner career in your next term.
You do not know what happened to you. There is a gap in your memory.

qualification.$rule: Core p.30: DEX or INT 5+ (engine takes better DM); DM-1 for every previous career.

entertainer.$assignments: Core p.30 Assignments + Survival & Advancement (cross-checked Career Summary Table p.14, ref lines 302-304).

skillTables.$rule: Core p.30-31 Skills and Training tables (Advanced Education printed min. EDU 10).

ranks.$rule: Core p.30-31 Ranks and Bonuses (three distinct per-assignment ladders).

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
You are invited to take part in a controversial event or exhibition. Roll Art or Investigate 8+. If you succeed, gain one SOC. If you fail, lose one SOC.
You are a part of your homeworld's celebrity circles. Gain one of Carouse 1, Persuade 1, Steward 1 or a Contact.
One of your works is especially well received and popular, making you a minor celebrity. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You gain a patron in the arts. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll and an Ally.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

You have the opportunity to criticise or even bring down a questionable political leader on your homeworld. If you refuse and support the leader, you gain nothing. If you accept, gain an Enemy and roll Art or Persuade 8+. If you succeed, gain one level in any skill you already have. If you fail, increase a skill anyway and roll on the Mishap table.
You go on a tour of the sector, visiting several worlds. Gain D3 Contacts.
One of your pieces of art is stolen and the investigation brings you into the criminal underworld. Gain one of Streetwise 1, Investigate 1, Recon 1 or Stealth 1.
As an artist, you lead a strange and charmed life. Go to the Life Events Table and have an Unusual Event.
You win a prestigious prize. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
You expose or are involved in a scandal of some sort.
Public opinion turns on you. Reduce your SOC by 1.

You are betrayed by a peer. One of your Contacts or Allies betrays you, ending your career. That Contact or Ally becomes a Rival or Enemy. If you have no Contacts or Allies, then you are betrayed by someone you never saw coming and still gain a Rival or Enemy.
An investigation, tour, project or expedition goes wrong, stranding you far from home. Gain one of Survival 1, Pilot 1, Persuade 1 or Streetwise 1.
You are forced out because of censorship or controversy. What truth did you get too close to? You gain DM+2 to the qualification roll for your next career.

qualification.$qualification: END 6+ (Core p.32). DM-1 for every previous career; DM-2 if you are aged 30 or more.

commission.$commission: SOC 8+ (Core p.32). Commission optional; first term only unless SOC 9+, DM-1 per term after the first.

marine.$assignments: Core p.32 Survival & Advancement by assignment; assignment skill columns from the Skills and Training tables.

skillTables.$rule: Core p.32 Skills and Training tables (Officer column commissioned only)

officer.title: Colonel
officer.benefit: SOC 10 or SOC +1, whichever is higher

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
Trapped behind enemy lines, you have to survive on your own. Gain one of Survival 1, Stealth 1, Deception 1 or Streetwise 1.
You are assigned to the security staff of a space station. Increase Vacc Suit or Athletics (dexterity) by one level.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to gain any one skill of your choice at level 1.

You are assigned to an assault on an enemy fortress. Roll Melee or Gun Combat 8+ and gain Tactics (military) or Leadership if you succeed. If you fail, you are injured and lose 1 point from any physical characteristic.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
You are on the front lines of a planetary assault and occupation. Gain one of Recon 1, Gun Combat 1, Leadership 1 or Electronics (comms) 1.

A mission goes disastrously wrong due to your commander's error or incompetence but you survive. If you report your commanding officer for their failure then you gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll and gain the officer as an Enemy. If you say nothing and protect them, gain them as an Ally.
You are assigned to a black ops mission. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
Your commanding officer takes an interest in your career. Either gain Tactics 1 or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
You display heroism in battle. You may gain a promotion or a commission automatically.

mishaps.text: Severely injured in action (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.

A mission goes wrong; you and several others are captured and mistreated by the enemy. Due to your injuries, you are discharged early. Gain your jailer as an Enemy and reduce your STR and DEX by one because of your injuries.
A mission goes wrong and you are stranded behind enemy lines. Increase Stealth or Survival by one level but, due to the mission's failure, you are ejected from the service.

You are ordered to take part in a black ops mission that goes against your conscience. If you refuse you are ejected from the service. If you accept you may stay with the marines but gain the lone survivor as an Enemy.
You are tormented by or quarrel with an officer or fellow marine. Gain that character as a Rival as they drive you out of the service.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

qualification.$rule: Core p.34; Career Summary Table p.21 - INT 4+; standard DM-1 for every previous career (applied globally by engine); no career-specific extra DM.

merchant.$assignments: Core p.34; Career Summary Table p.20 - Survival/Advancement cross-checked (Merchant Marine EDU 5+/INT 7+, Free Trader DEX 6+/INT 6+, Broker EDU 5+/INT 7+).

skillTables.$rule: Core p.34 Skills and Training tables

ranks.$rule: Core p.34 Ranks and Bonuses (three per-assignment ladders)

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

You are offered the opportunity to smuggle illegal items onto a planet. If you accept, roll Deception 8+ or Persuade 8+ to gain Streetwise 1 and an extra Benefit roll. If you refuse, you gain an Enemy in the criminal sphere.
Gain any one of these skills, reflecting your time spent dealing with suppliers and spacers: Profession 1, Electronics 1, Engineer 1, Animals 1 or Science 1.

You have a chance to risk your fortune on a possibly lucrative deal. You may gamble a number of Benefit rolls and roll Gambler 8+ or Broker 8+. If you succeed, you gain half as many Benefit rolls as you risked, rounding up. If you fail, you lose all the rolls risked. Either way, gain one level in whichever skill you used.
You make an unexpected connection outside your normal circles. Gain a Contact.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
You are embroiled in legal trouble. Gain one of Advocate 1, Admin 1, Diplomat 1 or Investigate 1, then roll 2D. If you roll 2, you must take the Prisoner career in your next term.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to increase any one skill you already have by one level.
A good deal ensures you are living the high life for a few years. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You befriend a useful ally in one sphere. Gain an Ally and either gain a level in Carouse or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
Your business or ship thrives. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
You are bankrupted by a rival. You lose all Benefits from this career and gain the other trader as a Rival.
A sudden war destroys your trade routes and contacts, forcing you to flee that region of space. Gain Gun Combat 1 or Pilot 1.
Your ship or starport is destroyed by criminals. Gain them as an Enemy.
Imperial trade restrictions force you out of business. You may take the Rogue career for your next term without needing to roll for qualification.
A series of bad deals and decisions force you into bankruptcy. You salvage what you can. You may take a Benefit roll for this term as well as any others you are entitled to.

merchant.$musterOut: Core p.35 Mustering-Out Benefits (Cash / Material Benefits, 1D).

qualification.$qualification: Core p.36 Qualification: INT 6+; DM-1 for every previous career; DM-2 if aged 34 or more

navy.$assignments: Core p.36 Assignments and Survival & Advancement table

skillTables.$rule: Core p.36 Skills and Training tables

officer.title: Captain
officer.benefit: SOC 10 or SOC +1, whichever is higher

officer.title: Admiral
officer.benefit: SOC 12 or SOC +1, whichever is higher

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

You join a gambling circle on board. Gain Gambler 1 or Deception 1. If you wish, throw Gambler 8+. If you succeed, gain an extra Benefit roll from this career; if you fail, you lose one Benefit roll from this career.
You are given a special assignment or duty on board ship. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to gain one level in any skill you already have.
Your vessel participates in a notable military engagement. Gain one of Electronics 1, Engineer 1, Gunner 1 or Pilot 1.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
Your vessel participates in a diplomatic mission. Gain one of Recon 1, Diplomat 1, Steward 1 or a Contact.
You foil an attempted crime on board, such as mutiny, sabotage, smuggling or conspiracy. Gain an Enemy but also gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll in the Navy.
You have the opportunity to abuse your position for profit. If you do so, gain an extra Benefit roll from this term. Refuse and you get DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
Your commanding officer takes an interest in your career. Either gain Tactics (naval) 1 or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
You display heroism in battle, saving the whole ship. You automatically pass your next promotion or commission roll.

mishaps.text: Severely injured in action (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
Placed in the frozen watch (cryogenically stored on board ship) and revived improperly. Reduce STR, DEX or END by 1 due to muscle wastage. You are not ejected from this career.

During a battle, defeat or victory depends on your actions. You must make an 8+ roll using a skill that depends on your branch: Electronics (sensors) or Gunner if you are crew, Mechanic or Vacc Suit if you are engineer/gunner and Pilot (small craft or spacecraft) or Tactics (naval) if you are flight. If you fail, the ship suffers severe damage and you are blamed for the disaster. You are court-martialled and discharged. If you succeed, your efforts ensure that you are honourably discharged. You still leave the career but may keep your Benefit roll from this term.

You are blamed for an accident that causes the death of several crew members. If you were responsible, then you gain one free roll on the Skills and Training tables before you are ejected from this career as your guilt drives you to excel. If you were not, then gain the officer who blamed you as an Enemy but you keep your Benefit roll from this term.
You are tormented by or quarrel with an officer or fellow crewman. Gain that character as a Rival, as they force you out of the Navy.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

musterOut.benefit: Ship's Boat or two Ship Shares

qualification.$qualification: SOC 10+. Automatic qualification if your SOC is 10 or higher. DM-1 for every previous career (standard rule, applied globally by the engine).

skillTables.$rule: Core p.38 Skills and Training tables

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table, but you are not ejected from this career.

You are challenged to a duel for your honour and standing. If you refuse, reduce your SOC by 1. If you accept, roll Melee (blade) 8+. If you succeed, gain one SOC. If you fail, roll on the Injury table and reduce your SOC by one. Either way, gain one level in Melee (blade), Leadership, Tactics (any) or Deception.
Your time as a ruler or playboy gives you a wide range of experiences. Gain one of Animals (handling) 1, Art 1, Carouse 1 or Streetwise 1.
You inherit a gift from a rich relative. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.

You become deeply involved in politics on your world of residence, becoming a player in the political intrigues of government. Gain one level in Advocate, Admin, Diplomacy or Persuade but also gain a Rival.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

A conspiracy of nobles attempts to recruit you. If you refuse, gain the conspiracy as an Enemy. If you accept, roll Deception 8+ or Persuade 8+. If you fail, roll on the Mishap table as the conspiracy collapses. If you succeed, Gain one level of Deception, Persuade, Tactics or Carouse.

Your reign is acclaimed by all as being fair and wise - or in the case of a dilettante, you sponge off your family's wealth a while longer. Gain either a jealous relative or an unhappy subject as an Enemy. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
You manipulate and charm your way through high society. Gain one level of Carouse, Diplomat, Persuade or Steward, as well as a Rival and an Ally.
You make an alliance with a powerful and charismatic noble, who becomes an Ally. Either gain one level of Leadership or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
Your efforts do not go unnoticed by the Imperium. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
A family scandal forces you out of your position. Lose one SOC.
A disaster or war strikes. Roll Stealth 8+ or Deception 8+ to escape unhurt. If you fail, roll on the Injury table.
Political manoeuvrings usurp your position. Increase Diplomat or Advocate by one level and gain a Rival.
An assassin attempts to end your life. Roll END 8+. If you fail, roll on the Injury table.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

qualification.$qualification: Core p.40: DEX 6+. DM-1 for every previous career (standard rule; no career-specific extra).

rogue.$assignments: Core p.40 Survival & Advancement (by assignment) + assignment specialist 1D skill columns (Thief/Enforcer/Pirate).

skillTables.$rule: Core pp.40-41 Skills and Training tables (Advanced Education printed min. EDU 10).

ranks.$rule: Core pp.40-41 Ranks and Bonuses. Thief and Enforcer have title-less ladders; Pirate has a titled ladder (Lackey to Captain). Civilian career - no officer ladder.

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

You are arrested and charged with a crime. You may defend yourself in court by rolling Advocate 8+: if you succeed the charges are dropped, but if you fail you gain an Enemy and must take the Prisoner career in your next term. Alternatively, you may hire a lawyer, gaining them as a Contact but losing one Benefit roll.
You are involved in planning an impressive heist. Gain one of Electronics 1 or Mechanic 1.
One of your crimes pays off handsomely. Gain DM+2 to any one Benefit roll from this career, but you gain your victim as an Enemy.
You have the opportunity to backstab a fellow rogue. If you take it, gain DM+4 to your next advancement roll. If you refuse, you gain them as an Ally.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).
You spend months immersed in the criminal underworld. Gain one of Streetwise 1, Stealth 1, Melee 1 or Gun Combat 1.
You become embroiled in a feud with a rival criminal organisation. Roll Stealth 8+ or Gun Combat 8+. If you fail, roll on the Injury table. If you succeed, gain an extra Benefit roll.

You get involved in a gambling ring. Gain Gambler 1. You may wager any number of your Benefit rolls and roll Gambler 8+; if you fail you lose the wagered Benefit rolls, but if you succeed you gain half the number wagered, rounding up.
A local crime lord takes you on as his protégé. Either gain Tactics (military) 1 or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to his aid.
You commit a crime that becomes the stuff of legend. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
You are arrested. You must take the Prisoner career in your next term.

Betrayed by a friend, ending your career. One of your Contacts or Allies betrays you and becomes a Rival or Enemy; if you have no Contacts or Allies you are betrayed by someone you never saw coming and still gain a Rival or Enemy. In addition, roll 2D; if you roll 2, you must take the Prisoner career in your next term.
A job goes wrong and you must flee off-planet. Gain one of Deception 1, Pilot (small craft or spacecraft) 1, Athletics (dexterity) 1 or Gunner 1.
A police detective or rival criminal forces you to flee your usual haunts and vows to hunt you down. Gain an Enemy.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

prisoner.$forcedOnly: Core p.52: the Prisoner career cannot be chosen; it is entered only when a mishap/event forces it. Excluded from the normal career-choice list.
prisoner.$parole: Core p.52: Parole Threshold starts at 1D+2 and is never above 12. At the end of each term, if the advancement roll (2D + advancement DM) exceeds the Parole Threshold the Traveller is released; otherwise they must serve another term. A prisoner cannot leave or be ejected otherwise (mishaps never eject, though their effects still apply).

qualification.$qualification: Core p.52: Special. No qualification roll - the Prisoner career is only ever entered when forced into it by another career's mishap/event.

prisoner.$assignments: Core p.56 Survival & Advancement (Inmate END 7+ / STR 7+; Thug STR 8+ / END 6+; Fixer INT 9+ / END 5+) + assignment specialist 1D skill columns.

skillTables.$rule: Core p.56 Personal Development and Service Skills (1D).
skillTables.$note: The Prisoner has NO Advanced Education table (advancedEducation null; advancedEducationEduMin null), like the Drifter (Core p.56).

ranks.$rule: Core p.56 Ranks and Bonuses: a single title-less ladder shared by all three assignments (0 Melee (unarmed) 1, 2 Athletics 1, 4 Advocate 1, 6 END +1). No officer ladder.
ranks.$note: As with every Mongoose career in this data (e.g. Army/Marine rank 0), the rank-0 benefit row is informational at entry: the engine applies a rank benefit only on promotion to that rank (ranks.ts promote), so the rank-0 Melee (unarmed) 1 is not auto-granted on entry. Melee (unarmed) remains reachable via the Inmate/Thug skill tables, service skills, personal development and events.

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table, but you are not ejected from this career.
You have the opportunity to escape. If you take it, roll Stealth 10+ or Deception 10+. If you succeed you leave this career immediately; if you fail, your Parole Threshold increases by 2.

You are put to hard, backbreaking labour. Roll END 8+. If you fail, your Parole Threshold increases by 1. If you succeed, your Parole Threshold decreases by 1 and you gain one of Athletics (any) 1, Mechanic 1 or Melee (unarmed) 1.

You are recruited into a prison gang. Roll Persuade 8+ or Melee 8+. If you fail, you gain an Enemy. If you succeed, your Parole Threshold increases by 1, you gain DM+1 to all Survival rolls for the rest of this career, and you gain one of Deception 1, Persuade 1, Melee (unarmed) 1 or Stealth 1.
Vocational training. Roll EDU 8+ to gain any one skill (other than Jack-of-all-Trades) at level 1.
Prison event. Roll 1D on the prison event sub-table.

entries.$rule: Core p.57 sub-event 1 (Riot): roll 1D - 1-2 injured, 3-4 nothing, 5-6 loot (extra Benefit roll).

events.text: A parole hearing goes in your favour. Your Parole Threshold decreases by 1.

You hire a lawyer to argue for your release (Advocate skill, at a cost of Cr1000 x their Advocate level squared). Roll 2D + the lawyer's Advocate 8+; if you succeed your Parole Threshold decreases by 1D.
events.$simplification: Core p.57 event 9. The optional Cr1000 x level^2 lawyer purchase and the hired lawyer's own Advocate are not modelled; the release check uses the character's own Advocate DM (0 if untrained) vs 8+ and, on success, applies modifyParoleThreshold with a die-string delta "-1D".

events.text: You are given special duties. Gain one of Admin 1, Advocate 1, Electronics (computers) 1 or Steward 1.
The warden takes an interest in your case. Your Parole Threshold decreases by 2.
You have the chance to act heroically and save a guard. If you take it, roll 2D: on 7 or less, roll on the Injury table; on 8+, gain an Ally and your Parole Threshold decreases by 2.

prisoner.$events: Core pp.56-57 Events (2D). modifyParoleThreshold/rerollParoleThreshold/rollSubTable are the Prisoner-specific effect kinds; parole increases are clamped to the parole.max of 12.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
You are accused of assaulting a guard. Your Parole Threshold increases by 2.

A prison gang persecutes you. You may fight back or lose all Benefit rolls gained from the Prisoner career. If you fight back, roll Melee (unarmed) 8+: on a failure, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result; on a success, you gain an Enemy and your Parole Threshold increases by 1.
A guard takes a dislike to you. You gain an Enemy and your Parole Threshold increases by 1.
You are disgraced and word reaches your homeworld. Reduce your SOC by 1.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

prisoner.$mishaps: Core p.57 Mishaps (1D). A prisoner is never ejected by a mishap (Core p.52); the mishap effects still apply.
prisoner.$musterOut: Core p.57 Mustering-Out Benefits (1D, rows 1-7). Rows 3/5 are a choice of one skill (" or "), row 6 a choice of one characteristic, row 7 grants all three listed skills (" and ").

qualification.$qualification: Core p.42 - INT 6+; DM-1 for every previous career

scholar.$assignments: Core p.42 Assignments; Survival & Advancement table (cross-checked Career Summary Table p.42)

skillTables.$rule: Core p.42 Skills and Training tables

ranks.$rule: Core p.42 Ranks and Bonuses (Field Researcher & Scientist share an identical title-less ladder 'research'; Physician differs; no assignment has rank titles)

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
You are called upon to perform research that goes against your conscience. Accept and you gain an extra Benefit roll, a level in each of any two Science skill specialties and D3 Enemies.
You are assigned to work on a secret project for a patron or organisation. Gain one of Medic 1, Science 1, Engineer 1, Electronics 1 or Investigate 1.
You win a prestigious prize for your work, garnering both the praise and envy of your peers. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You are given advanced training in a specialist field. Roll EDU 8+ to gain any one skill of your choice at level 1.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

You have the opportunity to cheat in some fashion, advancing your career and research by stealing another's work, using an alien device, taking a shortcut and so forth. If you refuse, you gain nothing. If you accept, roll Deception 8+ or Admin 8+. If you succeed, you gain DM+2 to any one Benefit roll and may increase any skill by one level but also gain an Enemy. If you fail, gain an Enemy and lose one Benefit roll from this career.
You make a breakthrough in your field. Gain DM+2 to your next advancement roll.
You become entangled in a bureaucratic or legal morass that distracts you from your work. Gain one of Admin 1, Advocate 1, Persuade 1 or Diplomat 1.
You work for an eccentric but brilliant mentor, who becomes an Ally. Either increase Science by one level or DM+4 to your next advancement roll thanks to their aid.
Your work leads to a considerable breakthrough. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
A disaster leaves several injured and others blame you, forcing you to leave your career. Roll on the Injury table twice, taking the higher result and gain a Rival.

The planetary government interferes with your research for political or religious reasons. If you continue with your work openly, increase Science by one level and gain an Enemy. If you continue with your work secretly, increase Science by one level and reduce your SOC by 2. This mishap does not cause you to leave this career.
An expedition or voyage goes wrong, leaving you stranded in the wilderness. Gain Survival 1 or Athletics (dexterity or endurance) 1. By the time you find your way home, your job is gone.

Your work is sabotaged by unknown parties. You may salvage what you can and give up (leave the career but retain this term's Benefit roll) or start again from scratch (lose all Benefit rolls from this career but you do not have to leave).
A rival researcher blackens your name or steals your research. Gain a Rival but you do not have to leave this career.

qualification.$rule: Core p.44 Qualification: INT 5+, DM-1 for every previous career (standard global rule; no career-specific extra). Cross-checked vs Career Summary Table Core p.21.

scout.$assignments: Core p.44 Assignments (Courier/Surveyor/Explorer) and Survival & Advancement table; cross-checked vs Career Summary Table Core pp.8-21.

skillTables.$rule: Core p.44 Skills and Training tables (Advanced Education min. EDU 8)

ranks.$rule: Core p.44 Ranks and Bonuses (single rank progression for all assignments; non-military, no officer ladder)

events.text: Disaster! Roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

Your ship is ambushed by enemy vessels. Either run and roll Pilot 8+ to escape, or treat with them and roll Persuade 10+ to bargain with them. If you fail the check, then your ship is destroyed and you may not re-enlist in the Scouts at the end of this term. If you succeed, you survive and gain Electronics (sensors) 1. Either way, gain an Enemy.
You survey an alien world. Gain one of Animals (riding or training) 1, Survival 1, Recon 1 or Science 1.
You perform an exemplary service for the scouts. Gain DM+1 to any one Benefit roll.
You spend several years jumping from world to world in your scout ship. Gain one of Astrogation 1, Electronics 1, Navigation 1, Pilot (small craft) 1 or Mechanic 1.
Life Event. Roll on the Life Events table (see page 46).

When dealing with an alien species, you have an opportunity to gather extra intelligence about them. Roll either Electronics 8+ or Deception 8+. If you succeed, gain an Ally in the Imperium and DM+2 to your next advancement roll. If you fail, roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.

Your scout ship is one of the first on the scene to rescue the survivors of a disaster. Roll either Medic 8+ or Engineer 8+. If you succeed, gain a Contact and DM+2 to your next advancement roll. If you fail, gain an Enemy.

You spend a great deal of time on the fringes of Charted Space. Roll Survival 8+ or Pilot 8+. If you succeed, gain a Contact in an alien species and one level in any skill of your choice. If you fail, roll on the Mishap table but you are not ejected from this career.
You serve as the courier for an important message from the Imperium. Either gain one level of Diplomat or DM+4 to your next advancement roll.
You discover a world, item or information of worth to the Imperium. You are automatically promoted.

mishaps.text: Severely injured (this is the same as a result of 2 on the Injury table). Alternatively, roll twice on the Injury table and take the lower result.
Psychologically damaged by your time in the scouts. Reduce your INT or SOC by 1.
Your ship is damaged and you have to hitch-hike your way back across the stars to the nearest scout base. Gain 1D Contacts and D3 Enemies.
You inadvertently cause a conflict between the Imperium and a minor world or species. Gain a Rival and Diplomat 1.
You have no idea what happened to you - they found your ship drifting on the fringes of friendly space.
Injured. Roll on the Injury table (see page 49).

scout.$musterOut: Core p.44 Mustering-Out Benefits (Cash + Material Benefits, per-career table)

mongoose.$careers: Core pp.22-45 twelve careers (Agent, Army, Citizen, Drifter, Entertainer, Marine, Merchant, Navy, Noble, Rogue, Scholar, Scout). Populated per-career in subsequent tasks.
mongoose.$agingStartTerm: Core p.49: ageing rolls begin at the end of the fourth term (age 34).
mongoose.$cashRollCap: Core p.46: may roll on the Cash column at most three times across all careers.
mongoose.$benefitsOfRank: Core p.46 Benefits of Rank: rank 1-2 -> +1 roll; 3-4 -> +2; 5-6 -> +3 and DM+1 to all Benefit rolls from this career.

pensions.$rule: Core p.49 Pensions: leaving after 5+ terms (except Scout/Rogue/Prisoner/Drifter) retires on a pension; Cr2000 per term beyond 8.

injury.text: Nearly killed - reduce one physical characteristic by 1D, reduce two other physical characteristics by 2.
Severely injured - reduce one physical characteristic by 1D.
Missing Eye or Limb - reduce STR or DEX by 2.
Scarred - reduce any physical characteristic by 2.
Injured. Reduce any physical characteristic by 1.

mongoose.$injury: Core p.49 Injury table (1D). Physical characteristics = STR/DEX/END.

aging.text: Reduce three physical characteristics by 2, reduce one mental characteristic by 1.
Reduce two physical characteristics by 2, reduce one physical characteristic by 1.
Reduce one physical characteristic by 2, reduce two physical characteristics by 1.

mongoose.$aging: Core p.49 Ageing table (2D - total terms), clamped to [-6, 1]. Reduce to 0 -> ageing crisis (die unless 1Dx Cr10000 medical care).

lifeEvents.text: Sickness or Injury: roll on the Injury table.
Birth or Death: someone close to you dies or is born; you are involved.
Ending of Relationship: a romantic relationship ends badly. Gain a Rival or Enemy.
Improved Relationship: a relationship deepens. Gain an Ally.
New Relationship: you become involved in a romantic relationship. Gain an Ally.
New Contact: you gain a new Contact.
Betrayal: a friend betrays you. Convert a Contact or Ally into a Rival or Enemy, otherwise gain a Rival or Enemy.
Travel: you move to another world. Gain DM+2 to your next qualification roll.
Good Fortune: something good happens. Gain DM+2 to any one Benefit roll.
Crime: lose one Benefit roll or take the Prisoner career in your next term.
Unusual Event: something weird happens. Roll 1D on the Unusual Event sub-table.

mongoose.$lifeEvents: Core p.46 Life Events (2D). Reached via an Events result of 7 on any career. Row 12 rolls 1D on lifeEventsUnusual.

lifeEventsUnusual.text: Psionics: you encounter a Psionic institute; you may test PSI and, if you qualify, take the Psion career next term.
Aliens: you spend time among an alien species. Gain Science 1 and a Contact among an alien species.
Alien Artefact: you have a strange device from an alien culture not normally available to humans.
Amnesia: something happened to you but you do not know what it was.
Contact with Government: you briefly came into contact with the highest echelons of the Imperium.
Ancient Technology: you have something older than the Imperium, or older than humanity.

mongoose.$lifeEventsUnusual: Core p.46 Unusual Event sub-table (1D on a Life Event of 12).
mongoose.$commissionAnyTermSocMin: Core p.19: a commission may only be attempted in the first term unless SOC 9+ (then any term).
mongoose.$skillLevelMax: Core p.19: a skill may never be increased beyond level 4 during creation; further increases are lost.
mongoose.$skillTotalCap: Core p.19: total skill levels may never exceed 3 x (INT + EDU).
mongoose.$connectionSkillCap: Core p.19 Connections rule: a Traveller may gain a maximum of two free skills, each connection with a different Traveller.
mongoose.$connectionSkillMaxLevel: Core p.19: a connection skill cannot be brought above level 3, nor may Jack-of-all-Trades be taken.

cashBonusSkill.$rule: Core p.46: Gambler skill grants DM+1 to Cash benefit rolls

mongoose.$connectionSkillExcluded: Core p.19: a connection skill may never be Jack-of-all-Trades.
mongoose.$qualificationDmPerPriorCareer: Core p.18: Qualification takes DM-1 for every previous career.
mongoose.$commissionDmPerTermAfterFirst: Core p.19: the Commission roll takes DM-1 for every term after the first.
mongoose.$agingDmPerTerm: Core p.49: the Ageing roll is 2D minus the character's total number of terms.
mongoose.$lifeEventsUnusualTrigger: Core p.46: a Life Event roll of 12 triggers the Unusual Event sub-table.
mongoose.$draftFallbackCareer: Core p.20: a Traveller who fails qualification after already being drafted becomes a Drifter.
mongoose.$survivalNaturalFail: Core p.18: a natural 2 on the Survival roll always fails.
mongoose.$advancementNaturalContinue: Core p.18: a natural 12 on the Advancement roll forces the Traveller to continue in the career.
